description:
You are acting as the sole data-engineer on call.  
Before tonight’s ETL job starts you must create a reproducible, checksummed backup of the current “raw” input data so that tomorrow’s job can be re-run from an immutable snapshot if needed.

The starting directory tree already exists:

/home/user/etl_project  
└── raw_data  
    ├── customers.csv  
    ├── orders.csv  
    └── products.csv  

Goal
1. Make sure there is a directory called /home/user/backups (create it if it does not exist).  
2. Create a compressed archive of /home/user/etl_project/raw_data named exactly  
   /home/user/backups/raw_data_20230115.tar.gz  
   The archive must be reproducible:  
   • Use the “-C” style path rewriting so that the files are stored as raw_data/*.  
   • Do NOT include absolute path prefixes.  
   • Every file inside the archive must have the deterministic modification time 2023-01-15 00:00:00 UTC, uid 0 and gid 0.  
     (Those three properties guarantee that anyone running the exact same command set will obtain an identical byte-for-byte archive, which is important for reliably reproducing the MD5 in step 3.)  

3. Generate an MD5 checksum for the finished /home/user/backups/raw_data_20230115.tar.gz and record both the size and the checksum in a human-readable single-entry log file.

   Log file path: /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt  
   The file must contain exactly four lines, in this order, each line terminated by a single newline character:

   DATE:<ISO-8601-instant-in-UTC>  
   ARCHIVE_PATH:/home/user/backups/raw_data_20230115.tar.gz  
   SIZE_BYTES:<integer number of bytes of the archive>  
   MD5:<32-character lower-case hex digest>  

   Example pattern (values will differ):

   DATE:2023-01-15T02:30:00Z  
   ARCHIVE_PATH:/home/user/backups/raw_data_20230115.tar.gz  
   SIZE_BYTES:12345  
   MD5:d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e  

   Precision requirements:
   • “DATE:” must be followed by a full UTC timestamp ending in “Z”.  
   • No spaces before or after the colons.  
   • No additional lines, blank lines, or trailing spaces anywhere in the file.

4. After writing the log, quickly sanity-check that the archive is readable by displaying a table-of-contents (you do NOT need to store that output anywhere).

Success criteria that an automated grader will check:
• /home/user/backups/raw_data_20230115.tar.gz exists, is non-empty, and when extracted recreates exactly the three CSV files under a single top-level raw_data/ directory.  
• /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt exists, contains precisely four newline-terminated lines matching the required keys in the required order.  
• The SIZE_BYTES value corresponds to the actual byte size of the archive.  
• The MD5 value equals the digest obtained from “md5sum /home/user/backups/raw_data_20230115.tar.gz”.  

Use only standard GNU/Linux user-space tools available in a minimal distribution (e.g. tar, mkdir, md5sum, echo, date). No root privileges are available. A typical solution requires about three or four commands.
truth:
Initial file system state (already present when the task begins):

/home/user/etl_project/raw_data/customers.csv
Contents (3 lines, unix LF):
id,name
1,Alice
2,Bob

/home/user/etl_project/raw_data/orders.csv
Contents (3 lines):
id,customer_id,total
101,1,19.95
102,2,5.00

/home/user/etl_project/raw_data/products.csv
Contents (3 lines):
id,description,price
501,Widget,9.99
502,Gadget,5.00

The directory /home/user/backups does not yet exist.

The automated test suite will:
1. Verify the directory /home/user/backups is created with default user permissions.
2. Confirm that /home/user/backups/raw_data_20230115.tar.gz exists and is non-empty.
3. Extract the archive into a temporary location and assert:
   • The only top-level entry is “raw_data”.
   • Inside raw_data/ there are exactly the three CSV files listed above.
   • The byte-for-byte contents of each CSV match the originals.
4. Read /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt and enforce:
   • It has exactly 4 lines.
   • Line 1 begins “DATE:” and the value matches regex
     ^DATE:20\d\d-\d\d-\d\dT\d\d:\d\d:\d\dZ$
   • Line 2 equals the literal required archive path line.
   • Line 3 begins “SIZE_BYTES:” followed by the archive’s actual file size.
   • Line 4 begins “MD5:” followed by 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters.
5. Recompute md5sum of the archive and compare to the value on line 4.
The test fails if any of these checks do not pass.
name: task_000000_7c548726
